content:
# Joshua Chapter 3 -- The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), folio pages 227-228 | Codex Vaticanus (LXX)
**Source Image:** `JOS_003_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_003_page_002.jpg`

---

### Israel Prepares to Cross the Jordan (vv. 1-6)

**1** And Joshua rose early in the morning, and they set out from Shittim and came to the Jordan -- he and all the Sons of Israel -- and they lodged there before they crossed over.

**2** And it came to pass at the end of three days that the officers passed through the midst of the camp,

**3** and they commanded the people, saying, "When you see the ark of the covenant of YHWH your God, and the Levitical priests carrying it, then you shall set out from your place and go after it.

**4** Yet there shall be a distance between you and it -- about two thousand cubits by measure. You shall not draw near to it -- so that you may know the way by which you shall go, for you have not passed this way before."

**5** And Joshua said to the people, "Consecrate yourselves, for tomorrow YHWH will do wonders in your midst."

**6** And Joshua spoke to the priests, saying, "Take up the ark of the covenant and cross over before the people." And they took up the ark of the covenant and went before the people.

---

### YHWH Speaks to Joshua (vv. 7-8)

**7** And YHWH said to Joshua, "This day I will begin to make you great in the eyes of all Israel, so that they will know that just as I was with Moses, I will be with you.

**8** And you shall command the priests carrying the ark of the covenant, saying, 'When you come to the edge of the waters of the Jordan, in the Jordan you shall stand.'"

---

### Joshua Announces the Living God (vv. 9-13)

**9** And Joshua said to the Sons of Israel, "Draw near here and hear the words of YHWH your God."

**10** And Joshua said, "By this you shall know that a living God is in your midst, and that he will surely drive out from before you the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Hivite, and the Perizzite, and the Girgashite, and the Amorite, and the Jebusite.

**11** Behold, the ark of the covenant of the Lord of all the earth is crossing over before you into the Jordan.

**12** And now, take for yourselves twelve men from the tribes of Israel, one man for each tribe.

**13** And it shall come to pass, when the soles of the feet of the priests carrying the ark of YHWH, the Lord of all the earth, rest in the waters of the Jordan, the waters of the Jordan shall be cut off -- the waters descending from above -- and they shall stand as one heap."

---

### The Crossing -- The Waters Stand Still (vv. 14-17)

**14** And it came to pass, when the people set out from their tents to cross over the Jordan, with the priests carrying the ark of the covenant before the people,

**15** and when those carrying the ark came to the Jordan, and the feet of the priests carrying the ark were dipped in the edge of the waters -- and the Jordan overflows all its banks throughout the days of harvest --

**16** the waters descending from above stood still and rose up as one heap, very far away, at Adam, the city that is beside Zarethan; and those descending toward the sea of the Arabah, the Salt Sea, were completely cut off. And the people crossed over opposite Jericho.

**17** And the priests carrying the ark of the covenant of YHWH stood firm on dry ground in the midst of the Jordan, while all Israel was crossing over on dry ground, until the entire nation had finished crossing over the Jordan.

---
## SOPHIA's Council Notes

All three translators demonstrated strong alignment across the seventeen verses of Joshua 3. The chapter's narrative is straightforward and the Hebrew text (Leningrad Codex, pages 227-228) is well preserved, with clear consonantal text and Masoretic vocalization visible in both folio images. The Vaticanus LXX column (page 164, visible in the manuscript image) corroborates the MT in all substantive readings, with only minor Greek translation choices diverging from the Hebrew structure.

**Manuscript verification:** The Leningrad Codex images confirm the standard MT readings across all seventeen verses. Key features verified include: the paragogic nun forms in verses 7 and 13 (ידעון, יכרתון); the infinitive absolute construction in verse 10 (והורש יורש); the Ketiv/Qere variant in verse 16 (מאדם / באדם); and the rare adverb הכן in verse 17. The Vaticanus LXX confirms the seven-nation list of verse 10, the title "Lord of all the earth" in verses 11 and 13, and the flood-stage parenthetical of verse 15. The LXX divergence at verse 16 (reading Kariathiarim for Zarethan) is noted but the MT reading is followed as the more geographically coherent tradition.

**Key consensus decisions:**

- **"Joshua" vs. "Yehoshua":** SOPHIA uses the anglicized "Joshua" following LOGOS and PNEUMA. EMET preserves the Hebrew transliteration "Yehoshua," which is faithful but departs from standard English Bible convention. The SOPHIA Bible uses standard English proper names for readability, reserving transliterations for the notes.

- **"Jordan" vs. "Yarden":** Same principle. SOPHIA follows LOGOS and PNEUMA with "Jordan." EMET's "Yarden" preserves the Hebrew but is unnecessary given that "Jordan" is universally recognized.

- **"Consecrate" (v. 5):** SOPHIA follows LOGOS ("consecrate yourselves") over EMET's "sanctify yourselves" and PNEUMA's "purify yourselves." All three are valid renderings of the Hitpael of qadash; "consecrate" best captures the ritual preparation sense while remaining accessible.

- **"Surely drive out" (v. 10):** The infinitive absolute construction (horesh yorish) required careful handling. EMET rendered it with a doubled phrase and bracketed notation ("certainly dispossess [drive out, drive out]"). LOGOS used "surely drive out." PNEUMA used "without fail drive out." SOPHIA adopts LOGOS's "surely drive out" as it preserves the emphatic certainty of the infinitive absolute in natural English, consistent with the translation rule requiring doubling to be represented.

- **"Lord of all the earth" (vv. 11, 13):** All three translators correctly distinguished Adon (lord/sovereign) from YHWH (the divine name), rendering this as "Lord of all the earth" rather than "YHWH of all the earth." Full agreement; no dispute.

- **Flood-stage parenthetical (v. 15):** EMET and LOGOS retain the parenthetical quality while differing in phrasing. PNEUMA restructures more freely. SOPHIA follows LOGOS's formulation as the best balance of fidelity and readability.

- **"Nation" vs. "nation" (v. 17):** All three translators correctly render haggoi as "nation," noting the significant shift from "Israel" / "Sons of Israel" to the collective singular. Full agreement on this theologically important term.

---
## Disagreement Log

See `/disagreements/JOS_003.md` for the complete disagreement log for this chapter.
